nodes.label: Headless by Default
nodes.rationale: No custom UI should be built unless absolutely necessary — keeps development effort low, reduces operator learning curve, and aligns with the invisible product vision. Clients never log into anything new.

nodes.label: Human-in-the-Loop Approval
nodes.rationale: Every external-facing action (login, apply, submit, send, spend, approve_status_change) requires explicit operator approval. Prevents wrong-opportunity submissions, boilerplate rejections, legal liability, and CAPTCHA/anti-bot issues. Operator retains full control.

nodes.label: Source-of-Truth Hierarchy (Precedence Rules)
nodes.rationale: When the same opportunity appears in multiple sources, My Opportunities (authenticated account state) always wins; API enriches but never overrides account state. Prevents stale Find results from corrupting live engagement state.

nodes.label: Protected Opportunities
nodes.rationale: Opportunities already in My Opportunities or Applications must not receive a new apply_to_principal approval. The approval gate blocks any submission for a protected ID to prevent duplicate applications and re-applying to vendors already engaged with.

nodes.label: Atomic-Write Pattern (_atomic_write_json)
nodes.rationale: Timestamped backup of existing file before overwrite; write to .tmp then replace(). Prevents a partially-failed or garbage run from silently overwriting a previous good run — the root cause of silent data loss in v6–v10.

nodes.label: Single-Navigation Pattern
nodes.rationale: Navigate once on the first query only, then reuse the settled page state for subsequent queries. Repeated sidebar clicks reset Ember.js SPA state causing viewport timeouts and 404 modals — separating navigation from extraction fixes this.

nodes.label: JS Click Fallback (_js_click)
nodes.rationale: When Playwright native click() fails because a button has scrolled out of the viewport, fall back to document.querySelector(...).click(). Required for the orange Search button when the Find Opportunities panel is below the fold.

nodes.label: Garbage-Filtering Rules
nodes.rationale: Find Opportunities occasionally returns error pages or SPA fragments captured as pseudo-cards. Filter entries with missing opportunity_id, title 'close' or empty, or 'There were errors' in full_text before reconciliation.

nodes.label: Syntaxis Labs HoldCo Structure
nodes.author: Hermes / commission-crowd-agent
nodes.rationale: Zero-Human-Corp hosts two distinct classes of projects (autonomous zero-human businesses vs human-operated ventures) with different governance, risk, and brand profiles. Syntaxis Labs HoldCo makes the structure intentional and legible without renaming the GitHub org (which would break forks, stars, CI webhooks, SSH remotes).

nodes.label: Approval Taxonomy
nodes.rationale: Six approval_action types (research_scoring, deeper_research, apply_to_principal, application_submitted, icp_campaign_draft, icp_campaign_send) gate distinct stage transitions. Every stage transition above application_approved requires explicit operator approval; no buyer outreach before vendor acceptance.

nodes.label: Opportunity Lifecycle State Machine
nodes.rationale: Linear state progression discovered → under_review → application_draft_pending → application_approved → application_submitted → principal_accepted/active. No reverse transitions. Terminal states prevent re-application. Source precedence resolves conflicting states across My Opportunities, Applications, Messages, Favourites, Find/API.

nodes.label: Micro-Agent Swarm Philosophy
nodes.rationale: Break intelligence into small single-purpose agents (Researcher, Writer, Scorer) instead of one giant prompt. Reduces hallucinations and improves quality by keeping context small per agent with few-shot examples from the client Config sheet.

nodes.label: Commission-Only Rep Application Model
nodes.rationale: Primary business model is independent commission-only sales representation, not buyer outreach. Principal first, ICP second: no buyer outreach is sent before the vendor accepts Syntaxis Labs as a rep; no ICP campaigns drafted before the accepted stage.

nodes.label: Local Supervisor Model Routing
nodes.rationale: Route supervisory decisions through local/Hermes-routed models instead of OpenAI API to eliminate API-billing, preserve network-isolation, and respect human-only approval gates. Task-type based routing: glm-5.1 for general review, qwen3-coder-next for code review, deepseek-v3.2 for reasoning, kimi-k2-thinking for draft review.

nodes.label: Human-Only Approval Gates (HUMAN_ONLY_VERBS)
nodes.rationale: Hard blocks on any AI-recommended action touching the outside world or spending: send_email, send_message, apply, login, call_api, spend_money, approve_status_change. BlockedActionChecker raises SupervisorBlockedActionError so AI cannot self-authorize sensitive actions.

nodes.label: Bounded Retry Decorator
nodes.rationale: Exponential-backoff decorator for network ops: max 3 attempts, 1–8s backoff, only retries httpx.TimeoutException and httpx.ConnectError. Prevents transient network failures from crashing discovery while avoiding infinite retry loops.

nodes.label: Payload Hashing (hash_payload)
nodes.rationale: SHA-256 hex digest (16 chars) of canonical JSON enables deduplication of identical outreach/application payloads. Combined with IdempotencyStore prevents duplicate CRM writes, approvals, and calendar events.

nodes.label: Idempotency Store
nodes.rationale: Bounded in-memory store (10k entries, LRU eviction) prevents duplicate CRM writes, duplicate approvals, duplicate calendar events. CRM add_lead scans existing rows by lead_id before appending and silently returns dedup=True on duplicates.

nodes.label: Approval Expiry (check_expiry)
nodes.rationale: Validates approvals against a TTL (default 7 days / 168 hours). Rejects expired or stale approval tokens so an old approval cannot be accidentally re-approved weeks later. Triple guard on approve(): re-approve blocked, reject-after-rejection blocked, expiry guard.

nodes.label: Dry-Run by Default
nodes.rationale: All commands default to --dry-run; explicit --write or --notify required for real execution. Combined with Google Sheet as source of truth and Supervisor Relay review of stale approvals, ensures no invisible local-only actions.
